words.definition: To examine something in detail in order to understand or explain it
words.exampleSentence: The researcher needed to analyze the data before drawing any conclusions.

words.definition: Important or large enough to be noticed or have an effect
words.exampleSentence: There has been a significant increase in global temperatures over the past century.

words.definition: A result or effect of an action or condition
words.exampleSentence: Many people are unaware of the environmental consequences of their daily choices.

words.definition: Despite what has just been said or referred to
words.exampleSentence: The experiment was difficult to set up; nevertheless, the team persisted.

words.definition: Mainly; for the most part
words.exampleSentence: The region is predominantly agricultural, with wheat as the main crop.

words.definition: Open to more than one interpretation; not clear
words.exampleSentence: The survey results were ambiguous and required further investigation.

words.definition: To become progressively worse
words.exampleSentence: Without proper maintenance, the historic building will continue to deteriorate.

words.definition: To rise and fall irregularly in number or amount
words.exampleSentence: Currency exchange rates tend to fluctuate based on economic conditions.

words.definition: A typical example or pattern of something; a model
words.exampleSentence: The discovery led to a fundamental paradigm shift in the field of physics.

words.definition: Present, appearing, or found everywhere
words.exampleSentence: Smartphones have become ubiquitous in modern urban life.

words.definition: To put a decision, plan, or agreement into effect
words.exampleSentence: The government plans to implement new policies to reduce carbon emissions.

words.definition: Prolonged public disagreement or heated discussion
words.exampleSentence: The new law has sparked considerable controversy among legal experts.

words.definition: To give special importance or prominence to something
words.exampleSentence: The professor emphasized the importance of critical thinking in academic writing.

words.definition: Certain to happen; unavoidable
words.exampleSentence: Some degree of economic disruption is inevitable during the transition period.

words.definition: A fact or situation that is observed to exist or happen
words.exampleSentence: The Northern Lights is a natural phenomenon that attracts tourists from around the world.

words.definition: To gather together or acquire an increasing number or quantity of
words.exampleSentence: Over time, small savings can accumulate into a substantial retirement fund.

words.definition: After a particular thing has happened; afterward
words.exampleSentence: The company reported losses and subsequently announced major layoffs.

words.definition: Including all or nearly all elements or aspects of something
words.exampleSentence: The report provides a comprehensive overview of current market trends.

words.definition: To make less severe, serious, or painful
words.exampleSentence: Planting more trees can help mitigate the effects of urban air pollution.

words.definition: Not easily convinced; having doubts or reservations
words.exampleSentence: Many scientists remain skeptical about the proposed cure for the disease.

words.definition: Sufficient for a specific need or requirement
words.exampleSentence: The report concluded that current safety measures were not adequate to prevent accidents.

words.definition: Very great or intense; having deep meaning
words.exampleSentence: The invention of the internet has had a profound impact on how we communicate.

words.definition: To damage or weaken gradually or insidiously
words.exampleSentence: Constant criticism can undermine a student's confidence in their abilities.

words.definition: A great difference between things
words.exampleSentence: The growing economic disparity between urban and rural areas is a concern for policymakers.

words.definition: Critical observation or examination
words.exampleSentence: The company's financial records came under close scrutiny during the audit.

words.definition: A proposed explanation made on the basis of limited evidence
words.exampleSentence: The researchers formulated a hypothesis about the relationship between sleep and memory.

words.definition: Stated clearly and in detail, leaving no room for confusion
words.exampleSentence: The instructions were explicit about the safety procedures to follow.

words.definition: Suggested though not directly expressed
words.exampleSentence: There was an implicit understanding that the meeting was confidential.

words.definition: Lying beneath or fundamental to something
words.exampleSentence: The underlying cause of the problem has yet to be identified.

words.definition: In spite of; despite
words.exampleSentence: Notwithstanding the challenges, the team managed to complete the project on time.

words.definition: Possible and practical to do easily or conveniently
words.exampleSentence: The engineers determined that the bridge design was technically feasible.

words.definition: A general agreement among a group of people
words.exampleSentence: There is now a broad scientific consensus on the reality of climate change.

words.definition: Denoting an action or event preceding or preparing for something fuller
words.exampleSentence: The preliminary results of the study are expected to be published next month.

words.definition: A system in which members are ranked according to relative status or authority
words.exampleSentence: The corporate hierarchy made it difficult for junior staff to voice their opinions.

words.definition: Important; famous; standing out so as to be easily seen
words.exampleSentence: She is a prominent figure in the field of neuroscience.

words.definition: A limitation or restriction
words.exampleSentence: Budget constraints forced the team to scale back their ambitious plans.

words.definition: The combination of ideas to form a theory or system
words.exampleSentence: The essay presents an impressive synthesis of existing research on the topic.

words.definition: The quality of being logically or factually sound
words.exampleSentence: Several experts have questioned the validity of the research methodology.

words.definition: Belonging naturally; essential
words.exampleSentence: The intrinsic value of education extends far beyond employment opportunities.

words.definition: Logical and consistent; easy to understand
words.exampleSentence: Students are expected to present a coherent argument in their final essays.

words.definition: The freedom to decide what should be done in a particular situation
words.exampleSentence: The manager used her discretion to approve the request without going through standard procedures.

words.definition: Based on observation or experience rather than theory or pure logic
words.exampleSentence: The theory is supported by empirical evidence gathered over decades of research.

words.definition: Extremely thorough, exhaustive, or accurate
words.exampleSentence: The admission process is rigorous and only the top candidates are selected.

words.definition: To find out something for certain; to make sure of
words.exampleSentence: The investigation aims to ascertain the exact cause of the accident.

words.definition: A set of reasons or a logical basis for a course of action
words.exampleSentence: The CEO explained the rationale behind the company's decision to relocate.

words.definition: To make something greater by adding to it; to increase
words.exampleSentence: The university decided to augment its library collection with more digital resources.

words.definition: Tending to cause harm
words.exampleSentence: Lack of sleep can be detrimental to both physical and mental health.

words.definition: To draw out a response, answer, or fact from someone
words.exampleSentence: The teacher used thought-provoking questions to elicit discussion among students.

words.definition: To extend the application of findings beyond the observed range
words.exampleSentence: We cannot extrapolate these results to the entire population without further study.

words.definition: To be or form a part of a whole; to make up
words.exampleSentence: International students constitute approximately twenty percent of the university's enrollment.

words.definition: To comply with rules, standards, or laws; to behave according to socially acceptable conventions
words.exampleSentence: All new buildings must conform to the latest safety regulations.

words.definition: To make or become less; to decrease in size, extent, or importance
words.exampleSentence: The medication helped diminish the severity of her symptoms over time.

words.definition: Mainly; for the most part
words.exampleSentence: The workforce in the technology sector is predominantly male.

words.definition: A basic structure underlying a system, concept, or text
words.exampleSentence: The research was conducted within a theoretical framework based on social learning theory.

words.definition: The ability to assess and begin things independently; a new plan or process
words.exampleSentence: The government launched a new initiative to promote renewable energy adoption.

words.definition: To show or prove to be right or reasonable
words.exampleSentence: How can you justify spending so much money on a single project?

words.definition: Laws, considered collectively
words.exampleSentence: New legislation was introduced to protect consumers from unfair business practices.

words.definition: A system of parts working together in a machine; a natural or established process
words.exampleSentence: The researchers identified the biological mechanism responsible for the disease.

words.definition: In spite of that; nevertheless
words.exampleSentence: The journey was long and tiring; nonetheless, they arrived in good spirits.

words.definition: Continuing; still in progress
words.exampleSentence: The investigation into the cause of the fire is still ongoing.

words.definition: A particular attitude toward or way of regarding something; a point of view
words.exampleSentence: The documentary presents the conflict from the perspective of local residents.

words.definition: A part, share, or number considered in comparative relation to a whole
words.exampleSentence: A significant proportion of the budget is allocated to education and healthcare.

words.definition: To strengthen or support, especially with additional personnel or material
words.exampleSentence: Teachers use repetition to reinforce key concepts in the classroom.

words.definition: Income, especially when of a company or organization and of a substantial nature
words.exampleSentence: The company reported record revenue of over ten billion dollars last year.

words.definition: To strengthen or support physically or mentally; to cause to continue for an extended period
words.exampleSentence: Can the planet sustain its current rate of population growth?

words.definition: The process or a period of changing from one state or condition to another
words.exampleSentence: The country is undergoing a difficult transition from a fossil fuel economy to renewable energy.

words.definition: The act of obtaining something; something bought or obtained
words.exampleSentence: Language acquisition in early childhood is a remarkable cognitive achievement.

words.definition: To publicly recommend or support a particular cause or policy
words.exampleSentence: Many environmentalists advocate for stricter regulations on industrial emissions.

words.definition: To distribute resources or duties for a particular purpose
words.exampleSentence: The committee decided to allocate additional funding to the research department.

words.definition: Persisting for a long time or constantly recurring
words.exampleSentence: Chronic stress can lead to serious health problems if left untreated.

words.definition: A raw material or primary agricultural product that can be bought and sold
words.exampleSentence: Oil remains one of the most valuable commodities in the global economy.

words.definition: To add to something in a way that enhances or improves it
words.exampleSentence: The two approaches complement each other, providing a more complete picture.

words.definition: To consist of; to be made up of
words.exampleSentence: The United Kingdom comprises four constituent countries: England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland.

words.definition: To form or devise a plan or idea in the mind; to become pregnant
words.exampleSentence: It is difficult to conceive of a world without instant communication.

words.definition: To keep or restrict someone or something within certain limits
words.exampleSentence: We should not confine our analysis to just one aspect of the problem.

words.definition: To be in conflict with; to assert the opposite of a statement
words.exampleSentence: The witness's testimony appeared to contradict the evidence found at the scene.

words.definition: To come or bring together for a meeting or activity
words.exampleSentence: The committee will convene next Monday to discuss the proposed changes.

words.definition: To recognize or ascertain what makes something different
words.exampleSentence: It is essential to differentiate between correlation and causation in scientific research.

words.definition: To make or become less
words.exampleSentence: Over time, the effectiveness of the treatment began to diminish.

words.definition: Written or spoken communication or debate
words.exampleSentence: Academic discourse requires a formal and objective tone throughout.

words.definition: To move something from its usual place; to force people to leave their homes
words.exampleSentence: Automation threatens to displace millions of workers in the manufacturing sector.

words.definition: The process of coming into existence or prominence
words.exampleSentence: The emergence of social media has transformed how people interact with news.

words.definition: An attempt to achieve a goal; a serious effort
words.exampleSentence: The space mission represents humanity's most ambitious scientific endeavor to date.

words.definition: Very large in size, quantity, or extent
words.exampleSentence: The project required an enormous amount of time and financial resources.

words.definition: A thing with distinct and independent existence
words.exampleSentence: The company was split into three separate legal entities after the merger.

words.definition: Equal in value, amount, function, or meaning
words.exampleSentence: One US dollar is roughly equivalent to 0.85 euros at current exchange rates.

words.definition: The gradual development of something over time
words.exampleSentence: The evolution of mobile phones from simple communication devices to powerful computers has been remarkable.

words.definition: To make full use of and derive benefit from a resource
words.exampleSentence: The company was accused of exploiting workers by paying them below the minimum wage.

words.definition: An irregular rising and falling in number or amount
words.exampleSentence: Seasonal fluctuations in temperature affect wildlife migration patterns.

words.definition: Forming a necessary base or core; of central importance
words.exampleSentence: Access to clean water is a fundamental human right.

words.definition: To produce or create something
words.exampleSentence: The new solar panels are expected to generate enough electricity for the entire building.

words.definition: A system of ideas and ideals, especially one forming the basis of economic or political theory
words.exampleSentence: The two parties represent fundamentally different political ideologies.

words.definition: To include or integrate a part into the whole
words.exampleSentence: The architect decided to incorporate elements of traditional design into the modern building.

words.definition: To bring about or give rise to; to cause
words.exampleSentence: Certain medications can induce drowsiness as a side effect.

words.definition: The basic physical and organizational structures needed for the operation of a society
words.exampleSentence: Investment in public infrastructure such as roads and bridges is essential for economic growth.

words.definition: A new method, idea, or product
words.exampleSentence: Technological innovation continues to reshape industries at an unprecedented pace.

words.definition: To combine one thing with another so that they become a whole
words.exampleSentence: The school aims to integrate technology into every aspect of the curriculum.

words.definition: To explain the meaning of information, words, or actions
words.exampleSentence: Different cultures may interpret the same gesture in completely different ways.

words.definition: To come between so as to prevent or alter a result or course of events
words.exampleSentence: The government had to intervene to prevent the bank from collapsing.

words.definition: A spoken or written account of connected events; a story
words.exampleSentence: The documentary presents a compelling narrative about the impact of climate change on coastal communities.
